fields.name: Ana Sagar Lake
fields.description: A beautiful artificial lake built by Anaji, grandfather of Prithviraj Chauhan in the 12th century.

fields.name: Foy Sagar Lake
fields.description: A scenic artificial lake built in 1891-92 as a famine relief project.

fields.name: Ajmer Government Museum
fields.address: Akbari Fort, Dargah Road, Ajmer, Rajasthan 305001
fields.description: Located in Akbar's palace, this museum houses Mughal and Rajput artifacts, sculptures and armory.

fields.name: Dargah Sharif (Ajmer Sharif)
fields.description: Famous shrine of Sufi saint Khwaja Moinuddin Chishti, one of the most sacred Muslim pilgrimage sites in India.

fields.name: Taragarh Fort
fields.description: One of the oldest hill forts in India, built in 1354 AD. Offers panoramic views of Ajmer city.

fields.name: Adhai Din Ka Jhonpra
fields.description: A mosque built in 1153 AD, one of the earliest examples of Indo-Islamic architecture in India.

fields.name: Nasiyan Jain Temple (Red Temple)
fields.description: A famous Jain temple also known as the Golden Temple, featuring golden dioramas of Jain cosmology.

fields.name: Akbari Fort & Palace
fields.description: Built by Emperor Akbar in 1570, this fort served as his residence during military campaigns.

fields.name: Mayo College
fields.description: One of India's most prestigious boarding schools, founded in 1875. Also known as the Eton of the East.

fields.name: MDS University Ajmer
fields.description: Maharshi Dayanand Saraswati University, a major state university serving Ajmer and surrounding districts.

fields.name: Subhash Udyan Park
fields.description: A well-maintained public park in the heart of Ajmer city, popular for morning walks.

fields.name: Company Garden
fields.description: A historic garden established during British colonial era, popular among locals and tourists.

fields.name: Ajmer Municipal Corporation (Townhall)
fields.description: The main civic administrative building of Ajmer city.

fields.name: Sophia School Ajmer
fields.description: One of the premier schools in Ajmer, run by the Congregation of Jesus.

fields.name: St. Anselm's School
fields.description: A well-known English medium school in Ajmer established by Christian missionaries.

fields.name: Pushkar Lake
fields.description: One of the five sacred dhams in Hinduism, this lake is surrounded by 52 ghats and 400 temples.

fields.name: Brahma Temple Pushkar
fields.description: One of the very few temples dedicated to Lord Brahma in the world, located in Pushkar.

fields.name: Savitri Temple
fields.description: Ancient temple dedicated to Goddess Savitri, located on top of Ratnagiri Hill in Pushkar.

fields.name: Ajmer Sharif Dargah Museum
fields.description: A small museum near the Dargah Sharif showcasing relics and history of the shrine.

fields.name: Government College Ajmer
fields.description: One of the oldest government colleges in Rajasthan, offering undergraduate and postgraduate courses.
